feedback.text: My entire life, 20 + years, I use games for pleasant time business. I often also play games with online activities. These games often contain a lot of money, namely for micro-transactions, so you can have more items in a game, or DLC (game extensions). It is therefore also a very strange idea that, despite me, I can stop hundreds of euros in a game, the publisher can close the game without notice and make sure that I spend my money practically enough. I would like to see legislation that goes against this. The only solution for me is that when I buy a game, it really remains with me, that is to say, developers make the game available for offline use at all times when they decide to stop supporting it.
